title: Pierre and Marie Curie University

infobox.value: [Sorbonne University](/wiki/Sorbonne_University_(group) "Sorbonne University (group)"),[[2]](#cite_note-2) [CNRS](/wiki/CNRS "CNRS"),[[1]](#cite_note-autogenerated1-1) [LERU](/wiki/League_of_European_Research_Universities "League of European Research Universities"), [EUA](/wiki/European_University_Association "European University Association")

(French: *Université Pierre-et-Marie-Curie* [[ynivɛʁsite pjɛʁ e maʁi kyʁi]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French"), **UPMC**), also known as **Paris VI**, was a [public](/wiki/Public_university "Public university") [research university](/wiki/Research_university "Research university") in [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), France, from 1971 to 2017.[[3]](#cite_note-upmc.fr-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) The university was located on the [Jussieu Campus](/wiki/Jussieu_Campus "Jussieu Campus") in the [Latin Quarter](/wiki/Latin_Quarter "Latin Quarter") of the [5th arrondissement of Paris](/wiki/5th_arrondissement_of_Paris "5th arrondissement of Paris"), France. UPMC merged with [Paris-Sorbonne University](/wiki/Paris-Sorbonne_University "Paris-Sorbonne University") into the new [Sorbonne University](/wiki/Sorbonne_University "Sorbonne University") in 2017.

(in French) [Sorbonne University – UPMC – University Pierre and Marie CURIE – Sciences and Medicine – Paris](http://www.upmc.fr/en/university/sorbonne_universities.html). Upmc.fr. Retrieved 16 June 2014.

**Pierre and Marie Curie University** (French: *Université Pierre-et-Marie-Curie* [[ynivɛʁsite pjɛʁ e maʁi kyʁi]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French"), **UPMC**), also known as **Paris VI**, was a [public](/wiki/Public_university "Public university") [research university](/wiki/Research_university "Research university") in [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), France, from 1971 to 2017.[[3]](#cite_note-upmc.fr-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) The university was located on the [Jussieu Campus](/wiki/Jussieu_Campus "Jussieu Campus") in the [Latin Quarter](/wiki/Latin_Quarter "Latin Quarter") of the [5th arrondissement of Paris](/wiki/5th_arrondissement_of_Paris "5th arrondissement of Paris"), France. UPMC merged with [Paris-Sorbonne University](/wiki/Paris-Sorbonne_University "Paris-Sorbonne University") into the new [Sorbonne University](/wiki/Sorbonne_University "Sorbonne University") in 2017.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pierre_and_Marie_Curie_University&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Paris VI was one of the inheritors of the faculty of Sciences of the [University of Paris](/wiki/University_of_Paris "University of Paris"), which was divided into several universities in 1970 after the student protests of [May 1968](/wiki/May_1968_events_in_France "May 1968 events in France"). In 1971, the five faculties of the former University of Paris (Paris VI as the Faculty of Sciences) were split and then re-formed into thirteen universities by the [Faure Law](/wiki/Edgar_Faure "Edgar Faure").
The campus of Paris VI was built in the 1950s and 1960s, on a site previously occupied by wine storehouses. The Dean, Marc Zamanski, saw the Jussieu campus standing as a tangible symbol of scientific thought in the heart of Paris, with the Faculty of Science, set in the Latin Quarter, as part of an intellectual and spiritual continuum linked to the university history of Paris. [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Paris 6 shared the Jussieu campus with the University of Paris 7 ([Paris Diderot University](/wiki/Paris_Diderot_University "Paris Diderot University")) and the Paris Geophysical Institute (*Institut de Physique du Globe*).

In 1974, the University of Paris VI adopted the name Université Pierre et Marie Curie, after physicists [Pierre](/wiki/Pierre_Curie "Pierre Curie") and [Marie Curie](/wiki/Marie_Curie "Marie Curie"). In 2006, Pierre and Marie Curie University entered into a partnership with the government of the [United Arab Emirates](/wiki/United_Arab_Emirates "United Arab Emirates") to create [Paris-Sorbonne University Abu Dhabi](/wiki/Paris-Sorbonne_University_Abu_Dhabi "Paris-Sorbonne University Abu Dhabi"), a spinoff in [Abu Dhabi](/wiki/Abu_Dhabi "Abu Dhabi"). In 2007, the university shortened its name to UPMC.[[3]](#cite_note-upmc.fr-3) In 2008 the university joined the association *Paris Universitas* changing its logo accordingly and adding the name of the association after its own.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

UPMC was a large scientific and medical complex in France, active in many fields of research with scope and achievements at a high level.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Several [university rankings](/wiki/College_and_university_rankings "College and university rankings") regularly put UPMC at the 1st place in France, and it had been ranked as one of the top universities in the world. The *[ARWU](/wiki/ARWU "ARWU")* in 2014 ranked UPMC as the 1st in France, 6th in Europe and 35th in the world and also 4th in field of mathematics, 25th in field of physics, 14th in field of natural sciences and 32nd in field of engineering, technology and computer science.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

UPMC had more than 125 laboratories, most of them in association with the [Centre national de la recherche scientifique](/wiki/Centre_national_de_la_recherche_scientifique "Centre national de la recherche scientifique") (CNRS). Some of its most notable institutes and laboratories include the [Institut Henri Poincaré](/wiki/Institut_Henri_Poincar%C3%A9 "Institut Henri Poincaré"), [Institut d'astrophysique de Paris](/wiki/Institut_d%27astrophysique_de_Paris "Institut d'astrophysique de Paris"), Laboratoire d'informatique de Paris 6 ([LIP6](/wiki/Laboratoire_d%27Informatique_de_Paris_6 "Laboratoire d'Informatique de Paris 6")), Institut de mathématiques de Jussieu (shared with [University Paris-Diderot](/wiki/Paris_Diderot_University "Paris Diderot University")) and the [Laboratoire Kastler-Brossel](/wiki/Laboratoire_Kastler-Brossel "Laboratoire Kastler-Brossel") (shared with [École Normale Supérieure](/wiki/%C3%89cole_Normale_Sup%C3%A9rieure "École Normale Supérieure")).

The university's Faculty of Medicine Pierre and Marie Curie is located in two [teaching hospitals](/wiki/Teaching_hospital "Teaching hospital"), [Pitié-Salpêtrière Hospital](/wiki/Piti%C3%A9-Salp%C3%AAtri%C3%A8re_Hospital "Pitié-Salpêtrière Hospital") and Hôpital Saint-Antoine (the latter itself being the successor to [Saint-Antoine-des-Champs Abbey](/wiki/Saint-Antoine-des-Champs_Abbey "Saint-Antoine-des-Champs Abbey")).

## Dissolution

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pierre_and_Marie_Curie_University&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Dissolution")]

In 2010, the [Sorbonne University group](/wiki/Sorbonne_University_group "Sorbonne University group") was created, including the [Pantheon-Assas University](/wiki/Pantheon-Assas_University "Pantheon-Assas University"), the [Paris-Sorbonne University](/wiki/Paris-Sorbonne_University "Paris-Sorbonne University"), the [Muséum National d'Histoire Naturelle](/wiki/Mus%C3%A9um_National_d%27Histoire_Naturelle "Muséum National d'Histoire Naturelle"), the [INSEAD](/wiki/INSEAD "INSEAD"), and the [University of Technology of Compiègne](/wiki/University_of_Technology_of_Compi%C3%A8gne "University of Technology of Compiègne"); for this occasion the UPMC logo were changed again.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

UPMC merged with [Paris-Sorbonne University](/wiki/Paris-Sorbonne_University "Paris-Sorbonne University") into a combined [Sorbonne University](/wiki/Sorbonne_University "Sorbonne University") on 1 January 2018.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)
